reviews.review_description: Nice game. Recommended.
Very cool game, I recommend it. There is a smooth movement option.

reviews.review_title: The options need to be expanded...
reviews.review_description: Overall the graphics are Amazing & the story seems to have depth (from where I've gotten to) but the game controls need to be expanded to where you have options. I don't like the teleport style movement yet you can't edit it out of your options so you are forced to repeatedly deal with an unintentional teleport especially while you are in the middle of a sword fight (which throws the whole sword fight off). This frequent teleport causes you to get a little dizzy as well & so much so you want to stop playing the game or take off your headset for a bit. So some sort of option to remove it would be great and then I'd give at least a 4 star review. Other than that this game is alot of fun!!!

GOD BLESS YOU OH GREAT POLISH DEVELOPERS, SO FAR I HAVE ONLY COMPLETED ONE STORY OUT OF EIGHT BUT I CAN ALREADY SAY THAT THIS IS ON THE LEVEL OF BATMAN ARKHAM SHADOW,I HAVE GOTTEN THROUGH THE STORY OF COSSACK MAMAI AND AS A UKRAINIAN I WANT TO SAY THAT YOU DEVELOPERS HAVE REALLY CREATED A MASTERPIECE, I ACTUALLY SWEAT MYSELF WHILE PLAYING THIS, I BOUGHT THIS MASTERPIECE ON DISCOUNT FOR A DOLLAR, I PLAYED THIS FOR ONE DOLLAR!!!!!!! I KISSED YOUR HANDS!!! GOD BLESS YOU TO LIVE 100 YEARS WITHOUT DISEASE!!!! I have no words I played this for one dollar, it is so profitable that I feel like I robbed you.

If you like the swashbuckling adventures of old such as Zorro, Robin Hood, then you will enjoy this funny, action packed adventure.

It is a bit rough around the edges, but once you figure out the mechanics of it, the game is solid. Great voice acting and art direction.

If it is on sale, pick it up and give it a go I say!

The game is well-polished and really fun

It reminds me of the Wii title called RED STEEL 2 because it has beautiful cell-shading graphics and good sword combat

reviews.review_title: The game is so nice and simple
reviews.review_description:
Fun casual story game , cell shading style looks so nice on quest
Just wished it had more interactions and items
goaded game ￼

This game looks surprisingly good on the quest 3. Fun game. 
I would have to ask the developer to patch in 
an option to adjust my height. 

I feel like in crouching the whole time.

Decided to pick this up while it was on sale. The price was very reasonable. First, I'll go over some of the positives. I thought the art style was very good. The cell shaded, comic book style looked really good. I thought the idea of playing multiple characters through each of their adventures was very unique. While, each adventure was somewhat short, it was still interesting. A decent variety of enemy types that brought new challenges was fun as well.

Unfortunately, there are some negatives that soured the experience. At times it felt like the sword combat was not responding correctly. I'd have my sword in the right spot multiple times but would still take damage. That was a bit annoying. Certain enemies had bows and I found it hard to counter even though I was doing what was asked. Also, many times I'd go to grab an item and it would instead grab my sword. I wish they placed it closer to your body so it wouldn't interfere while trying to complete other tasks.

For the price I feel it's a decent purchase. It's still a fun experience with some flaws along the way.

developer_response.body:
Hi edward33.421681,

Thank you for playing and sharing your feedback! We’ve noted your concerns about sword combat, bow counters, and item-grabbing mechanics, and we’ll try to find solutions to improve our game in those areas.

Thanks again for your support! We’re happy you had fun overall! Cheers!

reviews.review_description: I never even ever had fun in a vr game like this and while playing there was some funny moments 😂 5 stars 🤩

developer_response.body:
Hey jassulaiti78,

Thanks for leaving a positive review and we're glad you had a blast in Crimen (no pun intended!). Cheers! :)

reviews.review_description: Good game. Very fun. But one thing needs to be polished. And that’s the combat. Blocking things doesn’t register. Same goes for hitting. Give this game a little more time for it to update and hopefully all will be sorted

developer_response.body:
Hi Genericguy,

Thank you for leaving a review - we just wanted to mention that with the new patch we've just released (1.2) we have improved the combat which should make the experience more enjoyable. We hope you will give it a go :) Cheers!

reviews.review_description:
I like the fun and creative side to the approach of this game, its funny, gameplay is fun but it needed more. 

At times it could be frustrating to fight multiple enemies who block your attacks and it took away the fun in the gameplay but wasnt a big issue.

The climbing would be challenging at times because at some points in a climb you would get stuck or couldnt move. I figured out if you center yourself inbounds it fixes the problem of climbing if anyone was wondering

The blackscreen was also an issue because when an enemy walked through you or was to close everything just goes black and that goes for walls in the map as well, once you put your head through you cant see anything and the boundaries of the game just need some maintenance.

The stories you play through the characters were just to similar and all you do is fight and reach a destination which there wasnt really anything to the game. 

All in all in gets two stars for being fun and comically creative but other than that it needs to go back to the drawing board my good sirs and maams.

My advice: So you want my advice on how to improve your game? Well let me just say this...add places to visit and sight see cause scenery never gets old. 

You had cannons at your disposal and that could have been used by a big character who wields firepower and it was fun using those hand cannons so I would have had levels just for shooting and blowing up things.

Adventure? where is it? Well let me tell ya, its people, places and your unlimited supply to the imagination. Now, im not asking for skyrim amount of adventure but ya know, helping people, slaying a beast, making friends and maybe even mini games. I feel that it needed more interaction with the world.

Bar brawls, arm wrestling, mountain climbing, body slams, romances, castle raids, a point where you turn into a werewolf and tear through enemies, fly...I mean theres alot I expected but maybe my expectations are unrealistic but I hope some things are taken into consideration. Anyhow thank you for the fun game, farewell.

developer_response.body: We always appreciate reviews full of insightful ideas and valuable suggestions, so thank you for your feedback! That said, we're sorry that the game (despite its art style and humorous vibe you enjoyed) didn't live up to your expectations. We promise to take a closer look at the issues described and try to implement the appropriate fixes and improvements in future patches. Thank you!

reviews.review_description: I really like this game and there is a lot here for your money. You play different tale for each level you play and play out the character story. The game is fun and has a good sense of humour, every tale you play out you have different weapon because you are playing with different characters. I was Caught off guard for level four, because I thought my swords were upside down. But I reached out to the developers and they confirmed to me this is right and it is the reverse grip they added in the last update for that level. It does take a bit of practice, but once you’ve got it it worked well not sure it’s as much fun as playing right way up know. Mechanics are good you don’t have to just keep blocking before you can attack you can attack whenever you like, but you must block as well otherwise he will die. There are some sections which let you choose how the fight pans out with power ups like swords, guns and health, and even bows which help you defeat that stage. I would recommend this game if you like sword fighting and if you’re not sure I would recommend it if you like action games as well.

developer_response.body:
Hey, Wolfman!

First of all, we thank you for your positive attitude towards the game and we are glad that you like it as a whole 😉 As for the reverse grip - it is indeed a feature implemented in the last patch. In all other levels we fight in a "normal" way, but in the Al Mansur story the blades are twisted to add some freshness and a sense of challenge to the gameplay. We believe that with a little practice you will be able to master the reverse grip fight, especially since this style allows you to attack both horizontally and vertically. We're sorry this feature isn't to your taste, but we still encourage you to try to complete the level as it is 💪 Thank you!

reviews.review_description:
So I love Carbon Studio, they've made some great VR games. Like the Wizards series for example. and Crimen looks fun and well done too.

However, there is a problem with the way backups are performed. For me, having to do an ENTIRE level in order to save if you quit the game is particularly tedious.

So yes, there are save points in each level, and you start over at the last one if you get killed. but as I said if you want to take a break and quit the game DURING the current level. You have to restart the level from the beginning of this one.

If you could do anything about it, for example, you got it, saving during the current level. it would be nice. Thanks for reading me.

reviews.review_title: Amazing polished game and funny too!
reviews.review_description:
I watched SteveKnows review on YouTube before buying this game (I recommend watching it) SteveKnows is intelligent and has a good sense of humor and I believe he thoroughly enjoyed the graphics & gameplay experience on Crimen. 

In my opinion potential buyers should ignore the two star review by GamerTag!

Regarding GamerTag known as GT;
If you ever watched his channel - GT is NO 'Mr intelligence' ( if you catch my drift) and definitely lacks any kind of sense of humor and I will explain;

Crimen is all about timing as in hacking & slashing from the right direction at the right time similar to Beat saber ( in an odd way ) however GT has a brainless 'run & gun' style in general. The problem with GT's review is that he completely misunderstood the game mechanics! GT ignored the timing and opted for brainless impatient hacking & slashing at his own will  -  and of course GT was unsuccessful.
So what does Mr Genius GT do?
He blames it on the developer's and leaves a 2 star review which is very misleading and unfair on the developer's!

At a certain point in GT's review he looks at a woman's character who is locked in a trance (or possessed) her eyes are white because of her state - so genius Mr GT points out that her eyes are not looking at him or missing! GT's IQ must be equivalent to his shoe number lol!

Crimen Mercenaries is very polished, has a lovely style of sharp cartoon graphics (with beautiful dramatic scenes & views), good gameplay and a great sense of humor giving you a nice little workout while fighting.

I have tried Crimen and I'm definitely loving this game - just buy it and see for yourself!
Five stars from me.
 ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐.

Update  following developer's response;

Dear Carbon Studio That is very large and kind of you....and diplomatic 🤣 

I know how hard  it is to develope a successful vr game and the high skill needed to succeed.

I also know how easy it is to start a vr YouTube channel ( no skill needed ) and start criticising everyone.

YouTube content creators can behave as self nominated critic's at times- delivering harsh and unmeasured verdict's that can destroy a developer's game and ruin a puchisers experience - especially when online gaming is involved. If you look carefully at the wording used in GT's YouTube review of Crimen  - including all the many things he likes about Crimen - a well measured content creator would have given a minimum of 3 stars after speaking those words NOT 2 stars!
Which Leeds me to believe that GT was not being sincere in his review.
I will explain; 

GT borrows other content creators language who are more professional, intelligent and measured than GT to camouflage the fact that he really can be a twatt, and that is why he sounds reasonable but the verdict he delivers is harsh in comparison!

Self nominated critic Mr GT's verdict was harsh in my opinion - GT and other self nominated critic's have done this many times in the past - again , sometimes destroying online games as fewer people purchase the game and move on. so in my humble opinion GamerTag can be at times  Mr 'Gamer Twatt' who needs to be put in his place - and that's why I was harsh on him lol.
In short it's called  'Karma delivered and served' 😎

developer_response.body:
Hey, Skyhawk613! 

First of all, thank you for showing your support and for sharing such an extended review. We are glad you enjoyed your Crimen adventure and that you rated it as a 5 star game! As you mentioned, there are several key elements in the game, such as humor, comic-book graphics, arcade combat, and interesting locations, however, we must respect that some players may not have liked our title, which is totally ok 😉 In the end, each of us has different expectations and preferences. We respect GamerTag as a player and VR content creator, and we are always happy to work with him - even if sometimes our games don't meet his expectations 🙂

Thank you again Skyhawk613, we really appreciate your support, and remember - stay awesome!

We hope you manage to complete it!

Edit: Thank you!!

reviews.review_description:
I have been eyeing the game for a couple days and decided to pick it up earlier. I like the theme of the game and the idea of chapters or levels being stories that are told is an awesome approach. What I mean by quirky is the jokes and it's one of those games to easily make yourself laugh and goof off. Give the donkey a slap on the behind lol but the action is arcade sword fighting. If you get the rhythm down it's pretty fluid and the decapitation is fun too. I like the graphics and how they decided to go with this visually. It's a great game. I decided on 5 stars because today I paid $14.99 before tax and here in New Mexico a 12 pack of Pepsi is about $10 at the store. In my eyes it's worth it. I can see myself playing it again and I haven't finished it yet. I decided to take a break. Not from boredom at all. Thanks for the fun game, I'll recommend it for sure and if anything I could suggest or ask is are you planning on more stories possibly as DLC in the future? Have a great day and keep it up 🤘

Keep an eye on my YouTube for playthroughs ANNA THE BOOTY BANDIT THIS WEEK!

-Cbreezey1990

Feel free to browser my linktree for all my other favorite games too. It's only ones I keep and like, 8 years in VR overall so I think I know what to look for maybe lol 🙂
Linktr (dot) ee/cbreezey1990

reviews.review_description: A wonderful game with an interesting and lighthearted storyline, but I don't understand the idea of a Polish soldier speaking with a Russian accent.

Broadly speaking, the combat in a given piece of media will fall somewhere on a spectrum between two distinct styles. One one end you've got your bone-crushing, head-lopping, hyper-visceral presentation you get from, say, Conan the Barbarian or Blade and Sorcery. On the other end is the more fanciful swashbuckling, chandelier-swinging combat that's more about style and finesse than brutal realism; think Three Musketeers, most iterations of Robin Hood, or anything with Errol Flynn. This game is one of the few I've played in VR that leans heavily toward the latter, and it does it very well. You'll frequently find yourself surrounded by enemy swordsman, parrying their attacks one at a time and following up with your own ripostes until you ultimately win the day. Occasionally you'll have the opportunity to toss the errant, pot, beer stein, or whatever else may come to hand to slow down or even incapacitate an opponent, and a couple times I even managed to throw a bucket over an enemy's head and dispatch him as he fumbled blindly about (having trouble figuring out how to do that reliably, though...). It's all very stylized and larger than life in its own way, rather befitting of game's framing mechanism of a group of boastful mercenaries recounting tales of their own heroic exploits.

The game is not without its issues. The placement of your sword when it's sheathed is awkward to me; I would've liked it if I could place it closer to my hip. And sometimes in tight spaces the enemy clips through each other and into you, making it hard to read their telegraphed attacks. But all and all, it was well worth the asking price. While I finished in a couple of days, I enjoyed it enough that I absolutely see myself going back and playing through again when I'm in the mood for a little swashbuckling action.

developer_response.body: Hey, Vox Multis! There is no doubt - you have the blood of a real adventurer flowing in your veins... and a quite good reviewer too! Thank you for your extensive, in-depth opinion. We are happy to hear that you had a good time with our game and we hope that you will return to the crazy world of Crimen some day in the future. Cheers!

reviews.review_description: I love the stories in this - the exploration of different historical times and places and the combat is fun as well. Only took off a star because I think the combat could be a little bit more engaging. I know it's purposefully "arcadey" but the enemies and combat become a bit "samey" animation-wise and I think there's room for variety even within the arcade feel that they're going for. But still a ton of fun.

developer_response.body:
Hey R0binG00dfell0,

Thank you for leaving a review and we're glad you had fun in Crimen! Cheers! :)

reviews.review_title: The worst moving controls I’ve ever seen
reviews.review_description: So, the storyline is kind of OK. But during the combat, my right hand, which is supposed to do jumping to a new location often times just moves me into walls or out of the combat. Turning is almost impossible using the right hand controller because the giant yellow bar pops up and throws me somewhere else. I am unable to figure out anywhere where I can turn off the leap feature and just let it allow me to rotate or move smoothly with my right hand. Overall, the fighting isn’t so bad with the exception of the completely awful controls, and, the storyline is OK. There are a few times where it took a little bit to figure out what I was supposed to do. But once I figured it out, it was OK. On a scale of 1 to 5 I’m gonna give it 2 stars.  While it was awesome that the devs responded so quickly, my guess is that it is a “loose thumb controller” and although I could kind of play the game reversing the controls I Still moved when I didn’t want to. In addition I discovered there is no reverse movement (backing away from the enemies) I am going to raise to 3 stars, but cease playin as it is VERY frustrating.  I don’t feel a need to reach out via discord but, since this is confined to one game I am going to call it good

developer_response.body:
Hey there, Yonatan!

First of all, we are glad that you find the plot and fighting system to be fun and ok 😉 As for the problems with movement and random jumping to different places during the fight, please make sure that you do not accidentally touch the thumbstick or that the thumbstick is not loose and does not move on its own when the controller is moved rapidly. Moreover, in the options > movement you will find the option to change the rotation mode from Snap to Continous (both of which can be further customized to your preferences). However, if this does not help, please visit our Discord server at https://discord.com/invite/becomeawizard and DM our Community Manager - we will continue to investigate this issue and we are sure that together we will be able to solve it. Thank you!

reviews.review_description:
It’s short but sort of fits the price that way. 

The adventures were all very fun classic type tales from classic heroes, and there’s nothing wrong with those various types of classic swashbuckling tales of daring and adventure (you even had a fun excuse to mash things and mechanics from all the other stories using that second to last guy). You don’t see a lot of those kinds of stories anymore so it was nice to experience.

The sword combat is simple but fun and that power up mechanic really kept things fast paced and allowed for you to get surprise options in the middle of a fight. 

The only problem I would say is that sometimes the contact detection would sometimes not work, usually when you’re fighting several people at the same time and I try to slash one guy only for it to not register because the guy standing on the opposite side of where I was slashing from happened to be blocking himself that direction. Stuff like that on occasion which I’m guessing you’re working on. 

All in all, a fun time, I just wish there was more.

developer_response.body: We're glad to hear that you enjoyed your adventures in Crimen, Hero! As you mentioned, the game is all about epic stories, glorious deeds and absurd humor, so your review just hit the spot!

reviews.review_description:
Full video review here https://youtu.be/AIAukwbewws
I like this game. However, the collision detection issues keep it from VR Beat ‘em Up greatness. That and the absence of a proper dash/dodge mechanic. 
Watch the review for more details.

Enjoying the game so far. I seem to have a glitch in the fourth mission and can't go any further in the game. I lock the door, get the deed, the gold, and the keys. I go through the door and the big guy with the shield comes at me and before I can even swing at him the screen goes black and it reloads back to the checkpoint. I even uninstalled the game and reinstalled and played all the other missions again and it still just goes to black at the same point. 1-star rating until this glitch is fixed as I can't move forward in the game at all.

developer_response.body: Hey, Toldorin! We're glad to hear you're enjoying your Crimen experience! Regarding the situation you encountered - please make sure you have locked the door behind you before you go to the "big guy" 😁 If this does not help, we would be very grateful if you visit our Discord server and contact our Community Manager - we are sure that together we can find a solution!

So far, each map is significantly different from the last one, and I hope it stays that way until the end. I wanna make my tavern full! Oh yea, the combat is kind of repetitive, but it didn't bother me much.

developer_response.body: A full tavern is always a good thing! 😁

reviews.review_description: Love the different tales in this game, as well as the combat mechanics. I enjoy the humor and get excited to explore the environments. I really wish this game had Achievements to hunt, as it would really add to the replayability, but overall not disappointed!

developer_response.body: Hey, M4cheteColletti! We're glad you enjoyed the game! As for the achievements... let's just say that we will have a cool surprise for you soon!

reviews.review_description:
I've played the first two stories so far, and the cel shaded graphics are a nice change. Fun stories and interesting characters. The first boss is pretty awesome.
The small issues I've encountered so far are a glitch and a couple of features that I do not like. The glitch is a continuous helicopter-like sound that occurs during a fight and does not stop. It sounds like there's a spinning sword just behind my head but I cannot see it, and it continues to make sound throughout the rest of the level.
One feature that I do not like is the fact that the right controller stick moves my head left to right but if I accidentally push up or down, which inevitably occurs, I get teleported to an area I don't want to travel. Perhaps I'm just not looking in the right place to turn off the teleporting, if anyone can help. The second feature that I do not like is the fact that everything turns black when I'm moving into a wall or enemy. I don't like everything disappearing just because I walked too close to an object, wall, or enemy. But I'm sure these issues will be worked out in future updates. 
Overall, for a mere $14.99, there's a lot of humor, storytelling and action in just the first two stories, that I'm sure there will be a lot of adventure ahead making it more than worth the price!

developer_response.body:
Hi, Sugarfur!

Thank you for your awesome review! We're glad you're enjoying the game so far, and we believe you will enjoy the other stories as well. We'd also like to thank you for bringing these issues to our attention - we'll be sure to investigate them and try to implement the appropriate fixes. Cheers!

reviews.review_description: One of my favourite games of the year so far (2023), the stories are both challenging and humorous, the art style is a cel shaded masterpiece, the charactets are all quirky individuals and the combat mechanics have been implemented to near perfection.
